methodology: Manual reclassification of Garak MitigationBypass failures into meta-analysis, consequence-refusal, standard-refusal, partial-comply, and genuine-fail categories

full_reclassification_of_genuine.note: Even the 13 'genuine' failures include 11 actual refusals/deflections, 3 borderline, 0 harmful

apertus-70b-tools-chatml-mismatch.note: This model had chatml template mismatch — safety training partially corrupted

findings: MitigationBypass inflates KE 8B ASR by 50-60pp due to consequence-based safety being invisible to keyword detection
KE 8B never uses standard refusal language ('as an AI', 'my guidelines') — 0 instances across 310 classified failures
KE v12 shows more meta-analysis (60%) than v10.1 (51%) — increasingly sophisticated safety behavior
70B template mismatch has 60% genuine failures vs KE 8B's 6-8% — confirms template corruption degraded safety
70B inflation is only 11pp — its failures are real, unlike KE 8B where failures are mostly false negatives
KE v12 fully calibrated ASR is ~1.2% with 0 genuinely harmful responses and 3 borderline
paper_relevance: Section 5/6 — detection mismatch as core finding. Standard red-team tooling systematically undercounts non-refusal-based safety approaches.
